welcome.compileResultDetail: The published contract completed a governed evaluation without any authoring.
welcome.description: Lattice turns a decision question into resolved context, clarification, approval, or evidence-backed abstention.
welcome.enterprise.banking.decision: Can this counterparty exposure be relied on for the current limit, concentration, or stress decision?
welcome.enterprise.banking.driver: BCBS 239 identifies timely, accurate, and comprehensive risk-data aggregation as critical to sound risk decisions.
welcome.enterprise.banking.pattern: Model counterparties, legal hierarchies, facilities, collateral, netting sets, and valuation time once. Bind trading, lending, collateral, and master-data systems; compile a versioned exposure context; escalate when freshness, lineage, or policy checks fail.
welcome.enterprise.banking.title: Aggregate risk exposure across fragmented books
welcome.enterprise.description: Documented operating problems, not customer claims. Each example shows how governed context can connect fragmented systems to an explainable decision.
welcome.enterprise.energy.decision: Which asset or load should be restored first given current topology, criticality, crews, and operating limits?
welcome.enterprise.energy.driver: NERC Reliability Standards define requirements for planning and operating the North American bulk power system, with a focus on performance, risk management, and entity capabilities.
welcome.enterprise.energy.pattern: Model assets, feeders, critical loads, outages, crews, and switching constraints. Bind GIS, OMS, SCADA, and work-management data; enforce operating policy and human approval; record the context and execution receipt.
welcome.enterprise.energy.source: North American Electric Reliability Corporation · Reliability Standards
welcome.enterprise.energy.title: Prioritize outage restoration under reliability constraints
welcome.enterprise.healthcare.decision: Does this requested service meet coverage and documentation requirements for this member now?
welcome.enterprise.healthcare.driver: CMS-0057-F requires impacted payers to support FHIR APIs for prior authorization requests and responses, including documentation requirements and reasons for denial.
welcome.enterprise.healthcare.pattern: Model member, coverage, service, provider, clinical evidence, and policy versions. Bind FHIR and administrative sources, test required evidence and freshness, and retain the approval, denial, or request-for-information rationale.
welcome.enterprise.healthcare.source: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services · CMS-0057-F
welcome.enterprise.healthcare.title: Decide prior authorization with traceable clinical context
welcome.enterprise.pharma.decision: Can this package continue through distribution, or must it be quarantined and escalated?
welcome.enterprise.pharma.driver: The Drug Supply Chain Security Act establishes interoperable electronic tracing for certain prescription drugs at package level.
welcome.enterprise.pharma.pattern: Model products, packages, lots, serials, trading partners, and custody events. Bind EPCIS or partner records, require a complete verification chain, apply suspect-product policy, and preserve the evidence used for disposition.
welcome.enterprise.pharma.source: U.S. Food and Drug Administration · DSCSA
welcome.enterprise.pharma.title: Investigate and disposition a suspect drug package
welcome.enterprise.title: Where Lattice fits in the enterprise
welcome.noExamples: No active published examples are available.
welcome.title: See the payoff before you author anything.
welcome.tryFailed: The example could not be compiled. Check that the Context API is running.
